name: supermedicine
description: Standalone Modular Medical Research Assistant with Optional OpenCode and Claude Code Adapter Entry Files

ai_provider.used_by: Claude Code optional invocation context through SuperMedicine runtime configuration

limitations: The standalone SuperMedicine core does not require Claude Code.
Local Claude Code CLI is optional and required only for claude.invoke.
OpenAI/Anthropic/OpenRouter provider configuration is supplied by installer/runtime/project config and must not be embedded as plaintext credentials.
No native Claude Code skill loading or native subagent dispatch is implemented.

opencode.native_skill_loading: skill documents provided; adapter can load local declared files
limitations: The standalone SuperMedicine core does not require OpenCode.
Only SuperMedicine is user-facing; alpha/beta/gamma/delta are internal role contexts.
AI provider configuration supports OpenAI, Anthropic, and OpenRouter API formats, custom BaseURL values, and redacted secret handling through installer/runtime/project config sources.
Task dispatch needs an injected SuperMedicine orchestrator/runtime bridge; otherwise it returns a degraded context result.

install_completeness_model.core_default: Python package and CLI run independently of assistant platforms.
install_completeness_model.platform_add_ons: OpenCode and Claude Code entries are optional metadata/adapter surfaces packaged under adapters/.
install_completeness_model.truthfulness_boundary: Adapter manifests declare only implemented local files, metadata, tool mappings, and degraded/unavailable states.

packaging_resources.exe_resource_strategy: Real SuperMedicine.exe binaries are release artifacts supplied via Install.py --release-exe and are not committed or packaged as source-tree data. Repository fixtures may use temporary test-created placeholder bytes only; durable placeholder policy/manifest files belong under installer/resources/ and must not use a .exe suffix.
packaging_resources.source_tree_exe_policy: Tracked .exe files are forbidden; generated desktop binaries are owned only when recorded under uninstall.recorded_artifact_keys such as binaries, binary_paths, shortcuts, or shortcut_paths.

cli.name: cli
cli.description: Command-line interface for SuperMedicine core runtime

tui.name: tui
tui.description: Terminal user interface for interactive sessions

install_steps.notes: Lowercase install.py is the canonical user-facing entrypoint; legacy Install.py remains compatible.

Default user path launches a concise four-step interactive wizard with no required -- flags: choose target directory/release extraction, initialize .supermedicine and LLM settings, choose optional shortcut/PATH/Desktop Exe actions, then review a clean confirmation summary with retry/cancel. Before release/init actions, the wizard detects existing installs by priority: .supermedicine/install-record.json, .supermedicine/config.yaml, payload collisions, then Desktop Exe collision. If found, it offers two primary choices (uninstall old version or update version) plus cancel; uninstall asks a second confirmation to preserve user data or remove recorded user data. Update preserves .supermedicine/config.yaml and user data by default, refreshes .supermedicine/install-record.json, and overwrites installer-owned payload/Desktop artifacts where requested. The wizard prompts for ordinary first-run parameters, shows defaults in brackets, hides API key input, rejects blank required LLM fields, validates Base URL as an http(s) address, writes config automatically, and avoids requiring users to hand-edit YAML or learn -- flags. SuperMedicineInstaller.exe uses the same wizard and, when frozen, defaults release payload extraction to yes. CI/scripted initialization can still use install.py --init (or legacy Install.py --init) with --provider <any-name> --base-url <url> --api-key <placeholder-or-secret> --model <model>, --llm-config <yaml>, or SM_LLM_PROVIDER/SM_LLM_BASE_URL/SM_LLM_API_KEY/SM_LLM_MODEL plus provider API key env vars. Scripted existing-install handling never prompts: pass --if-installed update|uninstall|fail|ignore. --if-installed uninstall requires exactly one of --preserve-user-data or --remove-user-data. Built-in defaults for openai, anthropic, openrouter. Missing provider/base_url/model/api key in scripted --init mode fails initialization and rolls back partial .supermedicine state; pass --interactive to prompt in --init mode. Use fake placeholders in shared docs; do not commit real keys. Installer output and install-record metadata do not include API keys. Existing --init semantics remain core initialization only unless --release-exe is explicitly provided. When running from a release archive, keep the extracted root intact so install.py/Install.py can import the sibling installer/ package; the Beta0.4.2 fixed layout includes installer/__init__.py and installer/exe_release.py next to both installer entry files.

Runs .supermedicine initialization and then desktop Exe release in one explicit installer mode. Requires the same complete LLM configuration as --init unless --if-installed update preserves an existing config.yaml. Legacy Install.py supports the same flags. Supports --if-installed update|uninstall|fail|ignore, --preserve-user-data/--remove-user-data for uninstall policy, --desktop-dir for controlled/test installs, --exe-target-name filename normalization, --exe-overwrite or default skip-if-exists behavior, and --exe-dry-run. Tests/CI should use --desktop-dir <tmp> or --exe-dry-run to avoid writing the real user Desktop. The release package must be a complete extracted directory; do not copy install.py or Install.py out of the archive.

Optional standalone release step: releases a specified .exe to the user's Desktop without initialization. Legacy Install.py supports the same flag. Supports --desktop-dir for controlled/test installs, --exe-target-name filename normalization, --exe-overwrite or default skip-if-exists behavior, and --exe-dry-run. This step does not inspect external platform config and must not include secrets. Run it from the extracted root that contains the installer/ package.
Secret-safe diagnostics for config load state, LLM readiness/missing fields, audit log path/writability, and repair commands including LLM list/switch, TUI dry-run, and Uninstall.py dry-run.

Safe local cleanup entry. By default it removes project-owned .supermedicine/runtime artifacts plus installer-created binaries, shortcuts, service/environment/PATH metadata, configuration/cache/log/temp directories and platform target paths recorded in .supermedicine/install-record.json or passed explicitly; it does not remove the source repository or pip package. Use --preserve-user-data only when user data should be retained.

uninstall.ownership_rule: Project-owned files are canonical SuperMedicine local directories/files inside the selected project root (.supermedicine, runtime artifacts, workspaces) plus installer-created binaries, shortcuts, configuration/cache/log/temp directories and platform target paths explicitly recorded in .supermedicine/install-record.json, install.json uninstall metadata, or passed via --target. Platform directories such as .opencode, .claude, and superpowers are never unconditional delete candidates. Outside-project paths, the source repository root itself, source files, and unrecorded user-owned global platform configuration are skipped.
uninstall.failure_audit: If cleanup cannot remove an artifact, PATH entry, environment variable, service, or registry residue, Uninstall.py returns status removed-with-residuals and includes residuals plus repair_suggestions.
opencode_artifacts: installer-recorded .opencode plugin, agent, skill, or capability files owned by SuperMedicine
